code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test28-APR-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with associated proteinuria and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria) may indicate renal or metabolic pathology, but no evidence of urinary tract infection. Low HbA1c suggests absence of chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible underlying causes of renal impairment.
